reasoning: The question asks whether the 30-day average of ACLED 'Battles' in Austria for periods before four resolution dates in 2025 will be higher than the 360-day average up to April 2025. The background notes that the 360-day average is strictly 0.000. For the question to resolve positively, there must be at least one 'Battle' in the 30 days prior to the resolution dates (April 20, May 13, July 12, October 10). ACLED defines battles as "violent interactions between two organized armed groups." In stable European democracies like Austria, battles are virtually non-existent; incidents of violence are generally categorized as terrorism/violence against civilians, riots, or isolated murders, none of which count as 'Battles' unless two organized armed groups clash (e.g., shootout between police and armed militia/cartel/terror cell). My searches revealed no ongoing or recent 'Battles' in Austria in the last few years, nor any emerging threats that suggest an imminent battle. Therefore, the base rate is extremely low. To account for Black Swan events or extreme uncertainty in the long run, I am setting the probabilities starting at 5% for the nearest date and slightly increasing with horizon.
